errors.criterion_text: Functional hyposplenism or asplenia, patient under long-term antibiotic prophylaxis for this reason

Calcineurin inhibitor-resistant after ≥6 months of adequate dosing, OR calcineurin inhibitor-intolerant with documented contraindications, OR inadequate response or disease relapse after treatment with ≥2 other immunosuppressive agents
Steroid-dependence or frequently relapsing disease, unless complete remission achieved
Muscle invasive bladder cancer that is cisplatin-ineligible or declined based on Galsky criteria
Continuous migraine (defined as an unrelenting headache) within 1 month prior to Screening Visit
No diagnosis of probable or proven invasive pulmonary infection with Mucorales according to the 2020 EORTC/MSGERC criteria
Oncologic patients at risk of neoplastic cachexia
Detection of elevated levels of EBV DNA, RNA or proteins in affected tissues OR elevated levels of EBV DNA in the peripheral blood
Any other condition that, in the opinion of the investigator, would make the patient unsuitable for enrollment or could interfere with the patient participating in the study

analysis: Overall accuracy is 72% (29/40 labeled criteria matched exactly, n=40 total labels, 0 unmatched due to criterion-text drift). The weakest verdict class is NOT_MET at 20% recall, meaning the matcher most often mislabels this ground-truth category as something else. 4 case(s) where the true label is UNKNOWN (vignette genuinely silent) were instead called MET or NOT_MET -- the matcher's most consequential failure mode, since it manufactures certainty the source data doesn't support. The remaining 7 error(s) are boundary calls between UNCERTAIN and a definite verdict, which is a lower-stakes miss than the UNKNOWN-to-definite case. Labels were hand-written from criterion text + vignette alone, blind to the matcher's own output, so this number reflects genuine matcher accuracy rather than label leakage.
